reasons: Direct probe of /stingray/api/gis with a Chrome User-Agent returned 200 OK with the {}&&{json} CSRF-prefixed payload
/stingray/do/location-autocomplete returned 403 from CloudFront — Surf at runtime should clear it
Community scrapers (reteps/redfin, dreed47/redfin) report 403s at scale, suggesting per-IP rate limiting more than a hard bot wall

protections.label: aws-cloudfront-waf
notes: AWS WAF marker present on homepage responses for both stdlib and Surf Chrome TLS
Some Stingray paths (location-autocomplete) return 403 from CloudFront for plain stdlib + UA but typically clear under browser-like fingerprint
Per-IP rate limiting reported by community scrapers; mitigate with cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter at conservative defaults (1 req/s)

candidate_commands.name: homes
candidate_commands.rationale: Stingray gis search is the primary entry point

candidate_commands.name: listing
candidate_commands.rationale: Listing detail composes initialInfo + aboveTheFold + belowTheFold

generation_hints: Strip the {}&& CSRF prefix from every Stingray JSON response before decoding
Use Surf with Chrome TLS fingerprint at runtime (UsesBrowserHTTPTransport)
Conservative rate limit: 1 req/s default with adaptive backoff on 429
Stingray is geo-restricted to US IPs; doctor command should warn non-US users
Region IDs are visible in redfin.com URL paths (e.g., /city/30772/TX/Austin); region type 6=city, 1=zip, 11=neighborhood

warnings.message: Stingray JSON responses are prefixed with the literal bytes '{}&&' as CSRF prevention. Generated client must strip them before json.Unmarshal.
Stingray endpoints are US-only. Non-US callers will get 403 regardless of TLS fingerprint.
